word_list.word: better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ tə hæv lˈʌvd ænd lˈɔst ðɐn nˈɛvɚ tə hæv lˈʌvd æt ˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to suggest that the joy and fulfillment of love, even if temporary, outweighs the pain of losing it

word_list.word: greater (love|life) hath no man (than this|)
word_list.pronunciation: ɡɹˈeɪɾɚ lˈʌv lˈaɪf hæθ nˈoʊ mˈæn ðɐn ðɪs

senses.definition: used to imply that the ultimate expression of love is to put the needs and well-being of another person above one's own, even if it requires personal sacrifice or hardship

examples.x: The firefighter rushed into a burning building to save a child, demonstrating that greater love hath no man.

word_list.word: hot love (is|) soon cold
word_list.pronunciation: hˈɑːt lˈʌv ɪz ɔːɹ sˈuːn kˈoʊld

senses.definition: used to imply that intense passion in a romantic relationship tends to fade quickly, highlighting the importance of building a relationship on a strong foundation of mutual respect and shared values

examples.x: While it's easy to get swept up in the passion and excitement of a new romance, the saying hot love soon cold, reminds us that building a lasting and meaningful relationship requires a strong foundation of mutual respect, understanding, and shared values.

word_list.word: love me little, love me long
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv mˌiː lˈɪɾəl lˈʌv mˌiː lˈɑːŋ

senses.definition: used to suggest that a long-lasting and steady love, even if it is not intense or passionate, is more valuable and fulfilling than a fleeting and intense love that burns out quickly

word_list.word: love begets love
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv bɪɡˈɛts lˈʌv

senses.definition: used to imply that when a person shows love and affection towards others, they are likely to receive love and affection in return

word_list.word: love and cough cannot be hidden
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv ænd kˈɔf kænˈɑːt biː hˈɪdən

senses.definition: used to imply that attempting to conceal feelings of love or affection is often unsuccessful, as they have a tendency to become apparent to others, just like the physical reflex of coughing

word_list.word: love is blind
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv ɪz blˈaɪnd

senses.definition: used to imply that love can make a person overlook or excuse flaws or shortcomings in their partner that they might otherwise find unacceptable

examples.x: The mother who always defended her child's behavior, even when it was clearly inappropriate, demonstrated that love is blind.

word_list.word: love sees no (faults|flaws)
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv sˈiːz nˈoʊ fˈɑːlts flˈɔːz

senses.definition: used to suggest that when a person is in love, they tend to overlook or forgive the faults and shortcomings of their loved one and see only their positive qualities

word_list.word: faults are thick where love is thin
word_list.pronunciation: fˈɑːlts ɑːɹ θˈɪk wˌɛɹ lˈʌv ɪz θˈɪn

senses.definition: used to suggest that when there is little love or emotional connection between people, they are more likely to focus on each other's flaws or shortcomings instead of offering support and understanding

examples.x: Sally's friend warned her that faults are thick where love is thin, after Sally complained at length about her boyfriend's habits and quirks.

word_list.word: love makes the world go (round|around)
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv mˌeɪks ðə wˈɜːld ɡˌoʊ ɹˈaʊnd ɐɹˈaʊnd

senses.definition: used to imply that love is a powerful and positive force that can bring people together, inspire acts of kindness and selflessness, and create a sense of community and belonging

examples.x: When the community came together to support those affected by the natural disaster, it was a reminder that love makes the world go round.

word_list.word: love me, love my dog
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv mˌiː lˈʌv maɪ dˈɑːɡ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of accepting and respecting the unique qualities and interests of others in relationships

word_list.word: the course of true love never did run smooth
word_list.pronunciation: ðə kˈoːɹs ʌv tɹˈuː lˈʌv nˈɛvɚ dˈɪd ɹˈʌn smˈuːð

senses.definition: used to imply that true love may encounter difficulties but remains strong and endures despite the challenges

examples.x: Even though they faced many challenges in their relationship, Mark and Lisa never lost sight of their love for each other, proving that the course of true love never did run smooth.

word_list.word: love laughs at locksmiths
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv lˈæfz æt lˈɑːksmɪθs

senses.definition: used to imply that when two people are in love, they will find a way to be together, regardless of any obstacles that stand in their way

examples.x: Even though they lived in different countries, the couple's love remained strong and they found a way to make it work, proving that love laughs at locksmiths.

word_list.word: love will (always|) find a way
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv wɪl ˈɔːlweɪz fˈaɪnd ɐ wˈeɪ

senses.definition: used to imply that when two people are meant to be together, their love will overcome any obstacles or challenges that may arise

word_list.word: love cannot exist where there is no trust
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv kænˈɑːt ɛɡzˈɪst wˌɛɹ ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ tɹˈʌst

senses.definition: used to emphasize the notion that trust is essential for building deep emotional connections and intimacy between individuals

word_list.word: there is always one who kisses, and one who turns the cheek
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz wˈʌn hˌuː kˈɪsᵻz ænd wˈʌn hˌuː tˈɜːnz ðə tʃˈiːk

senses.definition: used to imply that there is often an imbalance of affection or effort in any relationship or interaction, with one person giving more than the other

word_list.word: there is more pleasure in loving than in being (beloved|loved)
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz mˈoːɹ plˈɛʒɚɹ ɪn lˈʌvɪŋ ðɐn ɪn bˌiːɪŋ bɪlˈʌvd lˈʌvd

senses.definition: used for saying that experiencing the joy of loving someone brings greater satisfaction than being the object of someone's love

examples.x: I've had my fair share of heartbreak and disappointment, but I still believe that there is more pleasure in loving than in being beloved - it's a risk worth taking.

word_list.word: a loveless life is living death
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈʌvləs lˈaɪf ɪz lˈɪvɪŋ dˈɛθ

senses.definition: used to imply that a life without love and meaningful connections is empty and unfulfilling, similar to a state of emotional death or numbness

examples.x: The man had everything he could ever want, but still felt unhappy and unfulfilled, realizing that a loveless life is living death.

word_list.word: all the world loves a lover
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɔːl ðə wˈɜːld lˈʌvz ɐ lˈʌvɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that people are universally drawn to and admire those who are in love

examples.x: When Sarah and Jack got engaged, everyone was overjoyed, because all the world loves a lover.

word_list.word: every Jack has his Jill
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi dʒˈæk hɐz hɪz dʒˈɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that there is a perfect match for everyone, and every person will eventually find their ideal partner or soulmate

examples.x: When Sarah's friends teased her about her unusual tastes in music and art, she reminded them that every Jack has his Jill, and she would eventually find someone who appreciated her interests.

word_list.word: he that would the daughter win, (must|shall) with the mother first begin
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt wʊd ðə dˈɔːɾɚ wˈɪn mˈʌst ɔːɹ ʃˌæl wɪððə mˈʌðɚ fˈɜːst bɪɡˈɪn

senses.definition: used to imply that a man must win the approval of a young woman's mother if he wishes to pursue a relationship with her, emphasizing the importance of respecting family opinions in matters of love

word_list.word: hope is a lover's staff
word_list.pronunciation: hˈoʊp ɪz ɐ lˈʌvɚz stˈæf

senses.definition: used to imply that hope is a powerful force that helps people in love to endure difficulties and overcome obstacles, providing them with the strength and support they need to pursue their desires

examples.x: The couple faced many obstacles, but they never lost hope because they believed that hope is a lover's staff.
